back_translation: Paul, a servant of God, and an apostle of Jesus Christ, for the faith of those whom God has chosen, and for the knowledge of the truth that leads to devoutness toward God
in the hope of eternal life, which God who does not lie has promised since before the beginning of the eternal age
And at His own appropriate time, He has revealed His word through the proclamation, which I have been entrusted to keep, according to the command of God our Savior
To Titus, a true son in our common faith — may grace and peace from God the Father, and from Jesus Christ our Savior, abide with you
For this reason, I left you on the island of Crete, so that you would set in order the things still left undone, and appoint elders in each city, as I directed you
namely, if anyone is blameless, the husband of only one wife, having children who are believers, who are not accused of dissolute reckless behavior or insubordination
For the overseer must be blameless, as a steward of God — not stubborn-self-willed, not quick-tempered, not addicted to alcohol, not violent, not greedy for shameful gain
On the contrary, he must be hospitable, loving what is good, sensible, just, holy, and self-controlled
Holding firmly to the trustworthy word according to the teaching received, so that he can encourage others with sound teaching, and rebuke those who contradict
For there are many who are insubordinate, speaking nonsense and deceiving, especially those who come from the group of the circumcised
Who must be silenced, because they tear down whole families, by teaching things that should not be taught, for the sake of shameful gain
One of their own, who is a prophet of theirs, said, 'The Cretans are always liars, dangerous beasts, gluttonous and lazy people'
This testimony is true. For this reason, rebuke them strictly, so that they may have a sound faith
And will not be preoccupied with untrue stories of the Jews, or the commands of men who turn their back on the truth
For the pure, all things are pure; but for those who are defiled and unbelieving, nothing is pure at all; rather both their mind and their conscience are already defiled
They affirm that they know God, but by their actions they deny Him; being detestable, disobedient, and unfit for every good deed
